page: Microsoft Azure DC128es_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the DCEV6 family. It features 128 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance is configured with 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding an allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per virtual core.

This server configuration does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external network-attached storage, though the instance is designated as Premium Storage capable. There are zero integrated GPUs, making it unsuitable for hardware-accelerated graphics or parallel computing workloads. Additionally, the instance does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the DC128es_v6 offers a high-density allocation of dedicated vCPUs and memory resources on a virtualized platform, suitable for enterprise-scale workloads that do not require local disk storage or GPU acceleration. Appropriate workloads include large-scale databases, memory-intensive enterprise applications, and virtualization environments that rely on external premium storage volumes.
description: Microsoft Azure DC128es_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server instance from the DCEV6 family, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with 128 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 512.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage, requiring external premium storage solutions, and does not feature any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This configuration is designed for large-scale, memory-optimized workloads and enterprise applications that demand high CPU core counts and substantial memory capacity without the need for local physical disks or hardware acceleration. It offers a high-density allocation of dedicated vCPUs and memory resources on a virtualized platform, suitable for enterprise-scale workloads that do not require local disk storage or GPU acceleration.

tagline: A dedicated x86_64 server platform featuring one hundred twenty-eight virtual cores and five hundred twelve gigabytes of system memory.
